trail.val: F3Q26 BLOWOUT: rev ~$41.5B (vs $33.5B guide), non-GAAP GM ~85% (guide ~86% F4Q26); F4Q26 guide rev ~$50B / EPS ~$31; 16 SCAs (14 = $100B min floor) targeting >50% of revenue, locked to 2030; capex FY27 above mid-$40B

forward.note: Beat & raise; 35/42 analysts raised EPS (next-FY consensus $144.27 from $101.74 a month prior), stock still ~9x forward — re-rate the remaining catalyst.

trail.val: HBM TAM ~$35B (2025) → ~$100B by 2028 (~40% CAGR), pulled in two years vs prior

forward.note: Entire CY26 HBM supply incl. HBM4 sold out on price and volume.

trail.val: UBS: DRAM contract +35%/+30% q/q C4Q25/C1Q26; could be longest upcycle in 30 years
DB raised PT: DRAM tightness extends to 2028+, agentic AI drives memory intensity
conventional DRAM 30-40% QoQ in Q3 & Q4 (Redburn); StoneX base Q3 +8-13%/Q4 +3-8%, MU likely price outlier
UBS raises 2H26 base-line: DRAM 3Q26 +32% QoQ (was +17%) / 4Q26 +18%, after +67% in 2Q; DRAM undersupplied until at least 2Q28 (2027 sufficiency -13.6%, 'unprecedented over 30 years')
BofA: no earnings miss vs the Korea correction — conventional ASP +50-60% QoQ; global DRAM ASP +53%/+21%/+7% QoQ 2Q/3Q/4Q; >50% of conventional DRAM still off-LTA (price upside remains)

forward.note: Bull = durable up-cycle through CY27/2028 (UBS/BofA raising the pricing cadence); bear (ex-VP) = textbook top, normalizes within a year; long-dated China capacity the residual oversupply watch.

trail.val: FY26 capex raised to >$25B; FY27 to step up meaningfully, >$10B incremental construction capex y/y
hardest to add wafer: ~365K wpm flat 2026 → ~440K end-2027 (Idaho 1&2 + PSMC); MU grows faster than Koreans in 2028 (+21% YoY)

forward.note: US construction lead times the constraint; greenfield drives 2028 outgrowth.

trail.val: UBS: MU HBM bit-share ~21-22% in 2026E; HBM front-end cap 30/65/100 k-wpm (4Q 24/25/26E); HBM EPS $5/$7+ 2026/27
HBM end-2026 cap ~100K wafers, risk of drop to ~80K if HBM4 qual issues persist; HBM 2027 revised down 60→50bn Gb-eq

forward.note: HBM4 base-die redesign for speed parity the key execution watch vs Samsung/Hynix.

trail.val: UBS Arcuri Buy PT $1,625 (from $535), "path to >$100 EPS"; LTAs lock ~30% industry DDR bits, ~$400bn FCF C27-29E
GS: longevity underestimated; even bear-case DRAM -30%/2027 then -30%/2028 still leaves ~40% GM
MS: MU cheaper on 2028 numbers than SanDisk; DRAM 10/10 the primary AI bottleneck

forward.note: LTAs/SCAs (not pricing) the re-rating catalyst — convert equity into earnings-durability story.
